author: akuzia
text: Argument to change method name in decorator?

author: akuzia
text:
I wonder is there a convinient way to change method name? I've found some comments in Methods class, tryed to use decorators to change function `__name__` values. Is there a possibility to include kwargs to method decorator, lke in jsonrpc [add\_method](https://json-rpc.readthedocs.io/en/latest/dispatcher.html#jsonrpc.dispatcher.Dispatcher.add_method)?

```
from jsonrpcserver import method, serve


@method(name='generic.ping')
def ping():
    return "pong"


if __name__ == "__main__":
    serve()
```

so calls like:

```
{
	"id":"d3fa8fd5-259e-9e02-6d07-25dbf49e410f",
	"jsonrpc":"2.0",
	"method":"generic.ping"
}
```

will be possible?

author: bcb
text: Yes I agree we should add this.

author: akuzia
text: [@bcb](https://github.com/bcb) I've looked into sources, looks like this functionality already been implemented, but was removed not so long ago in [5020573](https://github.com/explodinglabs/jsonrpcserver/commit/502057335cfaa5c7fc7cc57293e89923d9a5f576), why?

author: bcb
text:
I can’t remember why this was done.

Happy for it to be added back, if you want to make a PR.
